---
title: "standardized"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-03-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(nhanesA)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(haven)
library(survey)
library(ggplot2)
dfs <- list(

demographics = list(
G = nhanes("DEMO_G"),
H = nhanes("DEMO_H"),
I = nhanes("DEMO_I"),
J = nhanes("DEMO_J")
),


conditions = list(
G = nhanes("MCQ_G"),
H = nhanes("MCQ_H"),
I = nhanes("MCQ_I"),
J = nhanes("MCQ_J")
),

mental = list(
G = nhanes("DPQ_G"),
H = nhanes("DPQ_H"),
I = nhanes("DPQ_I"),
J = nhanes("DPQ_J")
),

income = list(
G = nhanes("INQ_G"),
H = nhanes("INQ_H"),
I = nhanes("INQ_I"),
J = nhanes("INQ_J")
),


activity = list(
G = nhanes("PAQ_G"),
H = nhanes("PAQ_H"),
I = nhanes("PAQ_I"),
J = nhanes("PAQ_J")
)
)
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

cycle_labels <- c(
  G = "2011-2012",
  H = "2013-2014",
  I = "2015-2016",
  J = "2017-2018"
)

join_one_cycle <- function(cycle) {
  dfs$demographics[[cycle]] %>%
    left_join(dfs$conditions[[cycle]], by = "SEQN") %>%
    left_join(dfs$mental[[cycle]],     by = "SEQN") %>%
    left_join(dfs$income[[cycle]],     by = "SEQN") %>%
    mutate(cycle = cycle_labels[[cycle]])
}

cycle_data <- lapply(c("G", "H", "I", "J"), join_one_cycle)
names(cycle_data) <- c("G", "H", "I", "J")


clean_cycle <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      cardio = if_else(
        MCQ160B == "Yes" |
        MCQ160C == "Yes" |
        MCQ160D == "Yes" |
        MCQ160E == "Yes" |
        MCQ160B == "yes" |
        MCQ160C == "yes" |
        MCQ160D == "yes" |
        MCQ160E == "yes",
        1, 0
      )
    ) %>%
    select(RIDRETH3, RIDAGEYR, RIAGENDR, INDFMPIR, DMDEDUC2,SDMVPSU,WTINT2YR,SDMVSTRA, cardio) %>%
    na.omit() %>%
    filter(RIDAGEYR >= 20) %>%
    rename(
      race = RIDRETH3,
      age = RIDAGEYR,
      gender = RIAGENDR,
      income = INDFMPIR,
      education =  DMDEDUC2
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      race = case_when(
        race %in% c("Mexican American", "Other Hispanic") ~ "Hispanic",
        TRUE ~ as.character(race)
      ),
      education = case_when(
        education %in% c(
          "Less Than 9th Grade","Less than 9th grade",
          "9-11th grade (Includes 12th grade with no diploma)"
        ) ~ "Not High School Graduate",
        education %in% c(
          "High School Grad/GED or Equivalent","High school graduate/GED or equivalent",
          "Some College or AA degree",  "Some college or AA degree" 
        ) ~ "High School Graduate",
        TRUE ~ as.character(education)
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!education %in% c("Don't Know","Refused",  "Don't know"  )) %>%
    mutate(
      cardio = as.factor(cardio),
      education = relevel(as.factor(education), ref = "Not High School Graduate"),
      gender = as.factor(gender),
      race = relevel(as.factor(race), ref = "Non-Hispanic White")
    )
}
cycle_G=clean_cycle(cycle_data$G)
cycle_H=clean_cycle(cycle_data$H)
cycle_I=clean_cycle(cycle_data$I)
cycle_J=clean_cycle(cycle_data$J)
cycle_G <- cycle_G %>%
  mutate(cycle = "2011-2012")

cycle_H <- cycle_H %>%
  mutate(cycle = "2013-2014")

cycle_I <- cycle_I %>%
  mutate(cycle = "2015-2016")

cycle_J <- cycle_J %>%
  mutate(cycle = "2017-2018")
df <- bind_rows(
  cycle_G,
  cycle_H,
  cycle_I,
  cycle_J
)

```

```{r}
library(survey)
#Collapsed race for survey interaction models b/c survey-weighted interaction models with 5 race categories use too many parameters
df <- df%>%
mutate(race_collapsed = case_when(
race=="Non-Hispanic White"~"Non-Hispanic White",
TRUE~"Minority"
))
df$race_collapsed = relevel(as.factor(df$race_collapsed),

ref = "Non-Hispanic White" )
df_year2015=df %>% filter(cycle=='2015-2016')
df_year2015$tranform_age=scale(df_year2015$age,center=TRUE,scale=FALSE)
df_year2015$age_piece_1=pmax(0,df_year2015$tranform_age-median(tranform_age,na.rm=TRUE))
df_year2015$age_piece_2=pmax(0,-1.5)
df_year2015$age_group <- cut(
  df_year2015$tranform_age,
  breaks = c(-1.5, -.5, 0, .5, 1.5),
  include.lowest = TRUE
)
nhc = svydesign(
id =~SDMVPSU,
strata = ~SDMVSTRA,
weights = ~WTINT2YR,
data = df_year2015,
nest = TRUE
)

```

```{r}

model_base_svy = svyglm(cardio~education+ns(tranform_age,5)+gender+race+income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
model_base_svy2 = svyglm(cardio~education+tranform_age+age_piece_1+gender+race+income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
model_base_svy1 = svyglm(cardio~education+tranform_age+gender+race+income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
anova(model_base_svy1,model_base_svy)
summary(model_base_svy)
```

```{r}
model_base_svy <- svyglm(
cardio ~ education + race +s(age)  + gender + income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial()
)
summary(model_base_svy)

```

```{r}
model_int_gender_svy <- svyglm(
cardio ~ education * gender +bs(tranform_age,2)+ race+ income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial()
)
summary(model_int_gender_svy)

```




```{r}
library(svydiags)
res = svystdres(model_int_race_svy, "SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU", doplot = TRUE)
h = svyhat(model_int_race_svy,doplot = TRUE)
cd = svyCooksD(model_int_race_svy,"SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU",doplot = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(svydiags)
res = svystdres(model_base_svy, "SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU", doplot = TRUE)
h = svyhat(model_base_svy,doplot = TRUE)
cd = svyCooksD(model_base_svy,"SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU",doplot = TRUE)

```

```{r}
model_int_gender_svy
X = model.matrix(model_int_gender_svy)
rows_used = as.numeric(model_int_gender_svy$y)
w = weights(nhc)[rows_used]
svyvif(model_int_gender_svy, X[,-1], w,"SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU")
summary(model_int_gender_svy)


```


```{r}
library(svydiags)
res = svystdres(model_base_svy1, "SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU", doplot = TRUE)
h = svyhat(model_base_svy1,doplot = TRUE)
cd = svyCooksD(model_base_svy1,"SDMVSTRA","SDMVPSU",doplot = TRUE)
```
```{r}
X = model.matrix(cardio~education+gender +tranform_age+ race+ income,
data = df_year2015)
y=df_year2015$cardio
w=df_year2015$WTINT2Y
ridge=cv.glmnet(X,y,family="binomial",alpha=0,weights = w,standardize=TRUE)
coef(ridge,xvar="lambda")
```
```{r}
model_base_svy1 = svyglm(cardio~education+s(age)+gender+race+income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
model_base_svy1_gen = svyglm(cardio~education*gender+s(age)+race+income,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())

model_base_svy= svyglm(cardio~education,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
summary(model_base_svy1)
```

```{r}
library(broom)
library(dplyr)

or_data <- tidy(model_base_svy, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    OR = exp(estimate),
    lower = exp(conf.low),
    upper = exp(conf.high)
  )
or_data <- or_data %>%
  filter(term != c("(Intercept)"))

p1=ggplot(or_data, aes(x = OR, y = term)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Odds Ratio",
    y = "Predictor",
    title = "Forest Plot of Odds Ratios Base Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(
  "forest_plots.png",
  plot = p1,
  width = 12,
  height = 6.75,
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r}
model_base_svy= svyglm(cardio~education,
design = nhc,
family = quasibinomial())
summary(model_base_svy)
exp(coef(model_base_svy))
```

```{r}
est <- coef(model_base_svy)

se <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model_base_svy)))

lower <- est - 1.96 * se
upper <- est + 1.96 * se

table <- data.frame(
OR = exp(est),
CI_lower = exp(lower),
CI_upper = exp(upper)
)

round(table, 3)
est <- coef(model_base_svy1_gen)

se <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model_base_svy1_gen)))

lower <- est - 1.96 * se
upper <- est + 1.96 * se

table <- data.frame(
OR = exp(est),
CI_lower = exp(lower),
CI_upper = exp(upper)
)

round(table, 3)
```



```{r}
p1=ggplot(df_year2015,aes(x=age,y=cardio))+geom_point()+geom_vline(xintercept = mean(df_year2015$age,na.rm=TRUE),linetype="dashed")+geom_hline(yintercept = mean(df_year2015$age,na.rm=TRUE),linetype="dashed")
p2=ggplot(df_year2015,aes(x=age,y=cardio*age))+geom_point()+geom_vline(xintercept = mean(df_year2015$age,na.rm=TRUE),linetype="dashed")+geom_hline(yintercept = mean(df_year2015$age,na.rm=TRUE),linetype="dashed")
p1
p2
```
